value.found_quote: Permission profiles let you apply least-privilege boundaries to local commands Codex runs on your behalf. A profile is a named policy that combines filesystem rules, which define what commands can read or write, with network rules, which define which destinations commands can reach.
value.rationale: The page explicitly substantiates every element of the claim: (1) "Permission profiles" is the named feature; (2) they are "least-privilege" policies; (3) they are "named" (a profile is a named policy); (4) they combine "filesystem rules" and "network rules"; (5) they apply to "local commands Codex runs." The page also clearly marks the feature as "Beta" and under active development, making the lifecycle status 'beta'/'preview'.
